message:
style(cli): clippy cleanups — unwrap_or over unwrap_or_else, compile-time const assert

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/doctor_orphan_refs.rs
- scan_one_project: replace
  `count_refs(project_path).unwrap_or_else(|| findings.len())`
  with `count_refs(project_path).unwrap_or(findings.len())`. The
  default is a trivially-cheap `len()` call that doesn't allocate
  or touch I/O, so the closure form was pure ceremony; clippy's
  `unnecessary_lazy_evaluations` lint flagged it. No behaviour
  change.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs
- self_update_metadata_sink_rejects_oversize_before_extending_vec:
  hoist the `MAX_METADATA_BYTES <= 16 * 1024 * 1024` invariant
  check from a runtime `assert!` inside the test body to a
  compile-time `const { assert!(...) }` block. The constants are
  both `const`-visible, so Rust 1.83+ `const`-block evaluation can
  settle this at compile time and the test can't silently
  regress by reordering assertion state. Matches the
  "prefer compile-time over runtime checks when feasible"
  pattern used elsewhere in the crate.
